title: I just bought the boxed set for evangelion at goodwill
display_title: I just bought the boxed set for evangelion at goodwill
wikitext:
[]: I just bought the boxed set for evangelion at goodwill             Quote Post by evageek111 » Sat Apr 13, 2013 2:44 pm ...it was 10 dollars. Anyone know what the actual value of this item is? I know it's more than 10 bucks but how much more? It's missing the box itself but all the DVDs are in perfect condition. Photos here. http://imgur.com/a/FA9Km

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 13, 2013 4:15 pm I see completed listings for as low as $30 on eBay. On one hand, I'm glad you've got a copy of the show, but I also wish it weren't the old DVDs, their quality is lacking by today's standards. But still, it's an entire series for $10, which isn't a bad deal.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Apr 16, 2013 4:36 pm Based on the spines of Volumes 1 and 2, it looks like they the DVDs were bought one-by-one as singles, as the box set had slightly revised cover art for said volumes. As such, your copy of Vol. 1 is the inferior AIX pressing released originally in mid-2000, and not the remastered MOFC re-release from late 2001. It really doesn't affect the value (which I put at of $30-$35 on average) but it's an interesting factoid.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Apr 16, 2013 5:48 pm It also means you don't have the extended "director's cut" versions of eps 21-24 (unless they were bought later to replace the original disks).

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Apr 16, 2013 10:52 pm Think of it as the starter set to be built on if you so desire.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Thu Apr 18, 2013 2:32 pm If you got it for 10 bucks then it is a good deal.
